questions.question: Explain the relationship between POSIX and the Single UNIX Specification.
expectedPoints: POSIX is a set of IEEE standards for OS compatibility
SUS is a comprehensive standard maintained by The Open Group
SUS includes POSIX standards as a subset
UNIX certification requires SUS compliance, not just POSIX

questions.question: Discuss the importance of POSIX standardization in Unix system programming, including benefits for developers and users.
expectedPoints: Addresses historical fragmentation of Unix variants
Enables code portability across different systems
Provides consistent API and utility behavior
Reduces development time by eliminating platform-specific code
Allows skills transfer between different Unix-like systems
